title: Waste pickers now more structured with bright yellow trolleys
description: GEORGE NEWS - An initiative that aims to...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - An initiative that aims to formalise the role of waste pickers in George was launched last week Thursday 17 October. George Municipality, in collaboration with Henque Waste, EWASA, Polyco, and the Department of Environmental Affairs, officially launched the Waste Picker Integration Programme.

The idea is to make a transition from informal waste collection to a structured and sustainable programme.


> To mark this milestone in South Africa's waste industry, the municipality and Henque Waste handed over fifteen trollies to waste pickers during the launch.


The municipality invites all waste pickers who are interested in joining the programme to register on the official database.

Portfolio Councillor for Community Services Marchel Kleynhans expressed his excitement about the progress being made. "I’m thrilled to see the improvement in waste collection services through the formalisation of waste pickers," said Kleynhans.

"This initiative will significantly benefit the public by boosting recycling rates, providing financial gains for waste pickers, and promoting public health, safety, and environmental sustainability."

Municipality and Henque Waste handed over trollies to waste pickers during the launch.


> Reclaimers


Waste pickers, also referred to as reclaimers, earn their living by collecting recyclable materials from waste and selling them into the recycling value chain. They also salvage reusable items for personal use or resale, contributing to the circular economy.


> For generations, informal waste pickers have been crucial to South Africa’s waste management efforts. These individuals, locally known as reclaimers or "bag scratchers," collect, sort, and sell recyclables, removing tonnes of waste from urban streets and diverting it from municipal landfills.


Douw Bezuidenhout of Henque Waste highlighted the impact of waste pickers, saying they purchase nearly 82 tonnes of recyclables monthly from waste pickers. "The war on waste is larger than any of us can imagine, but together, we are stronger, and we can work towards a cleaner, more sustainable future."

To register on the municipal database, visit the George Municipality’s Community Services at 82 Meade Street and contact Yolanda Malithe programme’s registration coordinator at 044 802 2900.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
